title: What to Expect When Hiring a General Contractor in Westchester County, NY
excerpt: Thinking about a home renovation or addition in Westchester County? Here's what you need to know before hiring a general contractor — from licensing requirements to what questions to ask.
content:
Hiring a general contractor in Westchester County, NY is one of the most important decisions you'll make for your home. Whether you're planning a full-scale renovation, a home addition, or a kitchen remodel, the right contractor can mean the difference between a project that adds lasting value and one that causes stress and cost overruns.

In New York State, general contractors are required to be licensed and insured. In Westchester County specifically, contractors must hold a valid Westchester County Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) license. Before signing any contract, ask to see proof of both their license and their general liability insurance certificate. A reputable Westchester contractor will have these documents readily available.

Questions to Ask Before Hiring

When interviewing contractors in Westchester, ask the following: How long have you been working in Westchester County? Can you provide local references from similar projects? Are you a licensed architect as well as a contractor? The last question matters more than most homeowners realize — firms that provide both architecture and construction services under one roof can save significant time and money by eliminating the coordination gap between designer and builder.

Understanding the Scope and Cost

In Westchester County, home renovation costs vary widely depending on the scope of work, the age of the home, and the finishes selected. A full kitchen renovation in Scarsdale or Bronxville typically runs between $80,000 and $200,000. A primary suite addition can range from $150,000 to $400,000 or more depending on size and complexity. Getting multiple bids is wise, but be cautious of bids that come in significantly lower than others — they often reflect incomplete scopes or inexperienced labor.

Why Architecture and Construction Under One Roof Matters

Many Westchester homeowners begin a renovation by hiring an architect separately from a contractor, only to discover that the two parties have different interpretations of the drawings — leading to change orders, delays, and cost increases. A firm like House Architecture & Construction, PLLC eliminates this problem entirely. Our team manages both the architectural design and the physical construction, ensuring that the vision conceived on paper is executed exactly as intended in the field.
